questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She lost not only her wallet but also.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The accident ____ a review of safety protocols.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I am looking forward to meet you.
options: I am looking forward for meeting you.
I look forward to meet you.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
I am looking forward to meet you.
questions.solution: I am looking forward to meeting you.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government must ____ steps to control pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Hardly had he arrived than the meeting started.
options: He had hardly arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived then the meeting started.
Hardly he had arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.
questions.solution: Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.

questions.stem: She ____ a keen interest in geology.

questions.stem: Correct: The furniture in this room are expensive.
options: The furniture in this room are expensive.
The furnitures in this room are expensive.
The furniture in this room is expensive.
The furniture in this room were expensive.
questions.solution: The furniture in this room is expensive.

questions.stem: The company has ____ its production targets.

questions.stem: Correct: She is smarter than any student in the class.
options: She is the smartest than any student in the class.
She is smarter than any other student in the class.
She is smarter than any student in the class.
She is smarter than all student in the class.
questions.solution: She is smarter than any other student in the class.

questions.stem: She is ____ for her contribution to science.

The committee is divided in its opinions.
The committee were divided in their opinions.
questions.solution: The committee were divided in their opinions.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Quantization noise in fixed-point FFT: SNR improves by:
questions.solution: Each additional bit doubles dynamic range → 6 dB SNR improvement. Key consideration for fixed-point DSP implementation.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Anechoic chamber pyramidal absorber: works by:
questions.solution: Pyramids taper from air impedance to lossy material. Gradual transition minimizes reflection. Carbon-loaded foam absorbs.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Power-on reset (POR): ensures:
questions.solution: POR: holds processor in reset until supply stabilizes and oscillator starts. Prevents undefined operation.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Power analyzer: true RMS measurement requires:
questions.solution: True RMS: valid for non-sinusoidal. Requires bandwidth > 10× highest harmonic. Oversampling ensures accuracy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=47.0kΩ, Rin=22.0kΩ. Gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PCM: quantization levels L=256. Number of bits:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Edge-triggered vs level-triggered:
options: Edge: samples at clock edge (positive or negative). Level: transparent when clock high/low
questions.solution: Edge: master-slave or edge-triggered. Level: latch (transparent). JK MS: race.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Steady-state error for a step input to Type 0 system:
questions.solution: K_p = lim_{s→0} G(s). e_ss = 1/(1+K_p) for step. Type 1+: e_ss = 0 for step.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 13 kHz tone is sampled at 12 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |13 − 12| = 1 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equations predict that a time-varying electric field produces a:
questions.solution: Faraday/Ampère–Maxwell coupling of changing E and B fields gives rise to EM waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active filters — Sallen-Key:
questions.solution: Sallen-Key: voltage-controlled voltage source (VCVS). Q = ½. Butterworth, Chebyshev, Bessel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transmission line characteristic impedance Z₀:
questions.solution: For lossless line: Z₀ = √(L/C). Coax Z₀ = (138/√ε_r)·log₁₀(D/d). Microstrip formulas vary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT bias: Vcc=5V, Rb=330kΩ, Rc=2.2kΩ, β=200. Base current Ib:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equation ∇·E = ρ/ε₀ is Gauss's law for:
questions.solution: Gauss: divergence of E = charge density / permittivity. Flux through closed surface = Q_enc/ε₀.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=10.0kΩ, Rin=10.0kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Poynting vector represents:
options: Power flow density (S = E × H, W/m²)
questions.solution: Poynting: S = E × H (W/m²). Average power: P_avg = 0.5·Re(E × H*).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse amplitude modulation (PAM):
options: Amplitude of pulse train varies with modulating signal
questions.solution: PAM: natural (flat-top) or instantaneous. TDM: interleaved PAM samples from multiple channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Built-in potential V_bi for Si PN junction at 300K:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 14 kHz tone is sampled at 8 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |14 − 8| = 6 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 1 µH and C = 100 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drift current is caused by:
questions.solution: Drift: J_drift = q(nμ_n+pμ_p)E. Due to applied E-field.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Laplace transform of e^(-at)u(t) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 100 V is modulated by a 50 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electron mobility in Si at 300K:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Correlation of x(t) with itself (autocorrelation) at tau=0:
questions.solution: Rxx(0) = integral of |x(t)|^2 dt = energy. Maximum value of autocorrelation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiplexing — TDM:
options: Time Division. Each channel gets time slot. Synchronous (fixed) or statistical
questions.solution: TDM: digital. PSTN (E1/T1). Statistical TDM: variable slot allocation. Frame synchronization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Common-mode rejection ratio of ideal op-amp:
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: A_cm = 0, so CMRR = A_d/A_cm = ∞.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 10 W with a net antenna gain of 100 and a path loss factor of 10000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Kalman filter: assumes process and measurement noise are:
questions.solution: Kalman: optimal for linear systems with white Gaussian noise. Minimum mean-square error estimator for this case.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT configuration — common base:
options: High voltage gain, very low input impedance, no phase shift
questions.solution: CB: low Rin (~re). High f_T. Used in RF amplifiers, cascode.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lead compensator:
options: Adds phase lead. Improves PM, increases BW. Zero before pole
questions.solution: Lead: G_c(s) = K·(1+sTz)/(1+sTp), Tz < Tp. Max phase at ω_m = 1/√(Tz·Tp).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT: Vcc=9V, Rb=330kΩ, β=200. Collector current Ic:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase shift oscillator uses:
options: 3 or more RC sections (180° phase shift at oscillation frequency)
questions.solution: Phase shift: each RC gives up to 60° phase shift. 3 RC sections → 180° at f_osc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=100kΩ, R₁=1kΩ. Closed-loop gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 10-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp ideal characteristics:
options: Infinite gain, infinite input impedance, zero output impedance, infinite bandwidth
questions.solution: Ideal: V_+ = V_- (virtual short), I_in = 0. Practical: A_OL ~ 10^5-10^7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: System type and steady-state error:
options: Type 0: finite K_p error. Type 1: zero for step, finite for ramp. Type 2: zero for step/ramp
questions.solution: Static error constants: K_p, K_v, K_a. Higher type → better tracking, harder stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metastability in flip-flops occurs when:
options: Setup or hold time violations cause indeterminate output
questions.solution: Metastability: output oscillates or stays between 0 and 1. MTBF decreases with clock frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ADC types:
options: Flash (fast), SAR (moderate speed/resolution), Dual-slope (high resolution, slow), Sigma-Delta
questions.solution: Flash: 2^n comparators, parallel. SAR: binary search. Dual-slope: integrating, noise rejection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1010₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 3-to-8 decoder enable input allows:
questions.solution: Enable: chip select. Two 3-to-8 → one 4-to-16 decoder using enable lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Asynchronous counter (ripple):
options: Clock daisy-chained. Simple, but slow (propagation delay accumulates)
questions.solution: Ripple: each stage divides by 2. 2^n states. Glitches due to propagation delays.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 256 distinct quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karnaugh map minimization for f(A,B,C) = Σm(0,1,2,3):
questions.solution: K-map: m0,m1,m2,m3 form a quad in row A=0. f = Ā.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flip-flop — D:
options: Q follows D at clock edge. D = 'delay' or 'data'
questions.solution: D: D = J = K'. Used in registers, latches. D-latch: level-sensitive. D-FF: edge-triggered.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flip-flop — SR:
options: Set (S=1) → Q=1. Reset (R=1) → Q=0. S=R=1 invalid
questions.solution: SR: basic latch. Cross-coupled NAND or NOR. Race when S=R=1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Asynchronous input — synchronizer:
options: 2-FF synchronizer. Reduces metastability probability. Mean time between failures (MTBF)
questions.solution: Synchronizer: MTBF = exp(t_r/τ)/(f·t_w·f_d). τ = FF time constant. MTBF >> product life.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequential circuits use:
questions.solution: Sequential: output depends on current inputs AND past states (finite state machine).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-bit binary counter counts from:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 16 distinct quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1101₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RC low-pass: R=4.7kΩ, C=0.001μF. -3dB frequency:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ofdm subcarrier orthogonality means:
options: Peak of each subcarrier coincides with nulls of all others
questions.solution: OFDM: subcarrier spacing Δf = 1/T_symbol. Orthogonal over symbol duration. FFT-based modulation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 47 kΩ and Rin = 10 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.stem: AM with m=0.5, input SNR=30. Output SNR (envelope detector):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM power efficiency is:
questions.solution: η = m²/(2+m²). For m=1: η = 1/3 = 33.3%. Most power in carrier (wasted).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=47kΩ, R₁=1kΩ. Closed-loop gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse position modulation (PPM):
options: Pulse position varies relative to reference (constant width/amplitude)
questions.solution: PPM: better noise immunity than PAM (noise affects amplitude). Requires synchronization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=220kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Error control — CRC:
options: Cyclic redundancy check. Detects burst errors. Polynomial division
questions.solution: CRC: generator polynomial. Remainder = CRC checksum. Ethernet: CRC-32. Hardware efficient.

questions.stem: BJT: Vcc=10V, Rb=470kΩ, β=100. Collector current Ic:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: JFET characteristics:
options: Voltage-controlled device. Vgs controls Id. Very high input impedance
questions.solution: JFET: Vp (pinch-off). Idss (saturation current). Square-law: Id = Idss(1 - Vgs/Vp)².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Class AB amplifier:
options: Conducts slightly more than 180° (eliminates crossover distortion)
questions.solution: Class AB: bias at small standing current. Between A and B. Good linearity, moderate efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active filter order:
options: Each order: -20 dB/decade. 2nd order: -40 dB/decade. 4th order: -80 dB/decade
questions.solution: Q determines peaking. Butterworth: Q=0.707. Chebyshev: Q>0.707 (peaking).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The total voltage gain of two amplifier stages with gains A₁ and A₂ in cascade is:
questions.solution: Cascaded voltage gains multiply (or add in dB): A_total = A₁·A₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with a half-wave rectifier, a full-wave rectifier has:
questions.solution: Using both half-cycles, full-wave rectification gives smoother output and better efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The common-base amplifier has:
options: Low input impedance, high voltage gain, no phase shift
questions.solution: CB: Z_in ≈ r_e (low), high gain, good high-frequency response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An analog multiplier:
options: Produces output proportional to product of two inputs
questions.solution: Multiplier: V_out = K·V_x·V_y. Gilbert cell (translinear). Used in modulation, frequency doubling, AGC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Memory types — RAM:
options: SRAM (static, flip-flop, fast, low density). DRAM (capacitor, slow, high density, refresh needed)
questions.solution: SRAM: 6T cell. DRAM: 1T+1C. DRAM requires refresh (~64ms). SDRAM: synchronous.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fermi level in an N-type semiconductor is:
options: Closer to conduction band than valence band
questions.solution: N-type: E_F above intrinsic level (closer to E_C). P-type: E_F below intrinsic (closer to E_V).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Source coding — Huffman:
options: Variable-length code. Prefix code (no codeword is prefix of another). Optimal for given probabilities
questions.solution: Huffman: symbols sorted by probability. Short codes for frequent symbols. Entropy bound.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Resonant converter: ZVS achieved when:
options: Switch turns on when voltage across it is zero
questions.solution: ZVS: voltage across switch reaches zero before turn-on. Reduces switching loss. Important for high-frequency converters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 4-to-1 multiplexer has:
questions.solution: 2ⁿ inputs → n select lines. 4=2² → 2 select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serial communication — I²C:
options: Synchronous. 2 wires (SDA, SCL). Addressing (7/10 bit). Multi-master. Acknowledge
questions.solution: I²C: open-drain. Pull-up resistors. 100 kHz (standard), 400 kHz (fast). Each device has address.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Feedback types:
questions.solution: Negative feedback: reduces gain, increases bandwidth, stabilizes, reduces distortion.
